# Baerly Storage

Vendorless, causally consistent document database that runs as a
server-side `Db` (from `@baerly/server`) behind a `Verifier`
synthesized from `config.auth` (or supplied directly via the
adapter factory), with your data persisted in any S3-compatible
bucket (S3, R2, GCS, Minio). Browsers and other clients talk to it
over HTTP via the typed `@baerly/client`. The change-feed is a
long-poll on `/v1/since?cursor=<lsn>` — there is no client-side
polling of the storage layer.

Day-1 deploy targets, both first-class:

- **Cloudflare Worker** (R2 binding, `@baerly/adapter-cloudflare`).
- **Self-hosted Node** (S3 / R2 / GCS / Minio over HTTP,
  `@baerly/adapter-node`).

`create-baerly-storage` scaffolds either shape; see the module map in
[CLAUDE.md](./CLAUDE.md) for the package layout.

## Minimal shape

Every scaffold ships `auth: "none"` in `baerly.config.ts` so the
day-1 happy path works with zero env vars; the adapter synthesizes
a no-op verifier and pins every request to `config.tenant`. For
production, each scaffold's `AGENTS.md` "Going to production"
section documents two upgrade recipes: Pattern A (env-aware factory
`verifier:` override — CF Access on CF, JWKS on Node) and Pattern B
(flip `auth: "shared-secret"` and supply `SHARED_SECRET` via the
platform's secret store).

Server (Cloudflare Worker — `examples/minimal-cloudflare/src/server/index.ts`):

```ts
import { baerlyWorker } from "@baerly/adapter-cloudflare";
import config from "../../baerly.config.ts";

// baerly.config.ts ships `auth: "none"`. Override with `verifier:`
// when prod env vars (CF Access team domain / audience) are set.
export default baerlyWorker(() => ({ config }));
```

Server (Node — `examples/minimal-node/src/server/index.ts`):

```ts
import { baerlyNode, s3Storage } from "@baerly/adapter-node";
import config from "../../baerly.config.ts";

await baerlyNode({
  config,
  storage: s3Storage({
    region: "us-east-1",
    bucket: process.env.BUCKET!,
    accessKeyId: process.env.AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID!,
    secretAccessKey: process.env.AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY!,
  }),
}).listen(8080);
```

Client (browser / Node, hitting either server):

```ts
import { createBaerlyClient } from "@baerly/client";

const client = createBaerlyClient({ baseUrl: "" }); // same-origin

type Ticket = { _id: string; title: string; status: "open" | "closed" };

const { _id } = await client.collection<Ticket>("tickets").insert({
  title: "hi",
  status: "open",
});
const open = await client.collection<Ticket>("tickets").where({ status: "open" }).all();
```

`@baerly/client` requires `baseUrl`; it is a typed HTTP client, not
a peer of the kernel. All causal-consistency / fence-time logic
lives in the server-side `Db` + `ServerWriter`.

## Bolt onto an existing Cloudflare Worker

If you already have a `wrangler.jsonc` (from `wrangler create` or an
existing project), the one-command bolt-on is:

```sh
pnpm create @gusto/baerly-storage .
```

`create-baerly-storage` detects `wrangler.jsonc`, patches the R2 binding +
vars, seeds `.dev.vars`, appends `baerly-storage` to `package.json`,
and prints the `src/index.ts` snippet for you to paste. Every write
is idempotent — re-running is safe. Full reference:
[docs/guide/add-to-existing-cf-worker.md](docs/guide/add-to-existing-cf-worker.md).

## Toolchain

pnpm · vitest · TypeScript 7 (tsgo) · oxfmt · oxlint · rolldown

## Verification

Run `pnpm verify` (typecheck + lint). It is the only check guaranteed
green on `main` — see CLAUDE.md for the full verification ritual and
known-baseline test failures.

## Where to start

- [CLAUDE.md](./CLAUDE.md) — agent guidance, module map, conventions
- [docs/contributing/features.md](./docs/contributing/features.md) — feature → code map
- [docs/contributing/architecture.md](./docs/contributing/architecture.md) — lifecycle and module graph
- [docs/spec/sync-protocol.md](./docs/spec/sync-protocol.md) — protocol theory
- [docs/adr/](./docs/adr/) — architecture decisions
- [docs/guide/troubleshooting.md](./docs/guide/troubleshooting.md) — known issues
- [docs/contributing/conventions/](./docs/contributing/conventions/) — path-scoped conventions for src/, tests/, docs/

The public API entry point is `packages/server/src/db.ts` (the `Db`
class). Every public method has JSDoc with `@example`. Read those
before any markdown reference.
